question:
Read the passage and answer the question.

'His survival skills were keeping him alive, but he was starting to wonder if he would ever be rescued. He saw more than ten ships sail past him, but none of them seemed to have a clue that he was there.'

What does the phrase 'have a clue' mean in this passage?
choices: To have complete certainty about something.
To be unable to guess or know something.

question: Fill in the blank: The plan was a great ________. Everyone was surprised by how well it worked.
Choose the correct binomial: After a hazardous journey, they were relieved to get home ________.
Fill in the blank: If plants don't get enough sunlight, they ________. (zero conditional)

Read the text and answer the question.

'Think of the professional footballers who forget all about fair play in an attempt to win their side a penalty. Fortunately, stories demonstrating the better side of human nature sometimes hit the headlines.'

What does 'hit the headlines' mean?
choices: To be reported in the news and gain public attention.
To cause an accident during a sports event.
To be mentioned briefly in a book.

question: Choose the correct word: There's a slim ________ that you might succeed, so be prepared for rejection.
Fill in the blank: I wish I ________ that relationship when I had the chance. (past regret)
Choose the correct option: 'I needn't have worried because most people aren't judging anyone else.' This means ________.
choices: I did something, but it was necessary.
I didn't do something, but it was necessary.
I did something, but it wasn't necessary.
Something was possible, but I didn't do it.

question: Fill in the blank: If I'd invested a few weeks in finding out about my business idea, it would have really ________.
Choose the correct third conditional: If he had stayed at home, he ________ the celebration.
Fill in the blank: You ________ have had breakfast; then you wouldn't be hungry now.

Read the passage and answer the question.

'To try and take his mind off his situation he sang and read. However, the isolation took its toll on him. He admitted that he almost gave up hope, but the thought of seeing his parents again kept him going.'

What does 'kept him going' mean?
choices: Helped him continue despite the difficult situation.
Made him feel worse about his situation.
Helped him fall asleep every night.
Kept him moving in the right direction.

question: Choose the correct binomial: You must try to be flexible. There should be some ________ in any friendship.
Fill in the blank: If they hadn't made the North Coast 500, people ________ poorer. (mixed conditional)
Choose the correct option: 'I shouldn't have listened to my friend.' This sentence means ________.
choices: It was a good idea to listen, and I did it.
It was not a good idea to listen, but I did it.
It was not a good idea to listen, so I didn't do it.
Something was possible, but I didn't do it.

question: Fill in the blank: Ziad is not very good at basketball. He wishes he ________ taller!
Choose the correct phrase: A significant ________ of working online is that you can work from anywhere.
Fill in the blank: He took a digital presentation to the interview and they loved it. What a ________!

Read the passage and answer the question.

'Safely back home with his family, Aldi considered his options. His family lived in some hardship and his job was, from his perspective, relatively lucrative at $134 a month. However, having found himself drifting three times since he started the job aged 16, he and his family decided it simply wasn't worth the risk. From now on, he'll be staying firmly on dry land.'

What does 'considered his options' mean?
choices: He ignored the problem and moved on.
He thought carefully about what to do.
He asked his friends for help.
He decided to stay on the sea.

question: Choose the correct mixed conditional: If the organisers ________ this, they would have done things differently.
Fill in the blank: We ________ the whole weekend on that project; it was finished in a day.
Choose the correct option: 'Touch and go' means ________.
Fill in the blank: I am writing to enter the competition. I ________ responsible for running sessions in English for young learners.

Read the passage and answer the question.

'Although some people might disagree, I can't help feeling that all the examples mentioned reflect the basic goodness of the human being.'

Which function does this sentence perform?
choices: Introducing both sides of the argument equally.
Summing up a personal opinion after considering other views.
Providing a fact about human behaviour.

question: Choose the correct form: Ali did not pass his exams. If only he ________ harder last year.
Fill in the blank: Working in the travel business ________ to many young people who want to work as flight attendants.
Choose the correct option: 'Ultimately, there are arguments on both sides.' This phrase is used when ________.
choices: acknowledging that each side has valid points before concluding.

question: Fill in the blank: Looking back, perhaps I ________ anything, but I did, so it's too late now.
Choose the correct conditional: 'If he had looked at the map, he wouldn't be lost.' This is a ________.
Fill in the blank: I wish I ________ that job opportunity when it was offered to me. (past regret)

Read the passage and answer the question.

'The first two times he got off relatively lightly; he was drifting for a week, and the second time for two days. Those experiences sound terrifying enough, but on the third occasion he was alone in the ocean for 49 long days.'

How long was Aldi alone at sea on his third experience?
Choose the correct word: If you do sports, there's always a ________ of injury.
Fill in the blank: Besides ________ physically fit, I also enjoy cycling.
Choose the correct option: 'I wish I could stop eating crisps.' This means the speaker ________.
choices: stopped eating crisps in the past.

question: Fill in the blank: If she ________ an interesting person, the journalists wouldn't have interviewed her. (mixed conditional)
Choose the correct option: When writing a formal letter and you don't know the recipient's name, you end with ________.

Read the passage and answer the question.

'I am writing to enter the competition to win the opportunity to teach English in Cambodia for six months. It has also been my lifelong dream to visit the Koh Rong Island.'

What is the main purpose of this letter?
choices: To apply for a paid teaching position in Cambodia.
To enter a competition to win a chance to teach English in Cambodia.
To request information about a trip to Cambodia.
To complain about a competition entry.

question: Fill in the blank: Unless you ________, you're going to be late for the lesson.
Choose the correct option: 'If only Alia knew how I felt about it.' means Alia ________ know how the speaker feels.
Fill in the blank: ________ in addition to having experience, I also have relevant qualifications. (formal linking in a letter)
